title: Yeast 14C radiotracer uptake (methylammonium)
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dkpac","text":"To test the functionality and the kinetic properties of ammonium transporters, radiotracer experiments can used. 14C-methylammonium is used as a proxy.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: For uptake measurements, grow yeast cells to logarithmic phase in YNB medium supplemented with 3% glucose and 1 mM L-arginine.
Harvest cells at OD 600 of 0.6 to 0.8, wash, and resuspend in 40 mM potassium phosphate buffer, pH 7, to a final OD 600 of 8.
Five minutes before the uptake measurement, supplement cells with 6% glucose and incubate at 30ºC.
To start the reaction, add 450 µL of cell suspension to 400 µL of the same buffer containing 200 µM methylammonium and 10 nM 14 C-methylammonium (2.11 Gbq/mmol; Amersham).
After 0.5, 1, 2, and 4 min, withdraw 200 µL aliquots, dilute in 5 mL ice-cold potassium phosphate buffer containing 200 mM methylammonium, and filter through glass fiber filters (GF/C; Whatman).
Wash filters twice with 5 mL of cold deionized water and analyze by liquid scintillation spectrometry.
A cytosolic trans-activation domain essential for ammonium uptake by Loqué, D. et al. published in Nature on 20 Dec, 2006 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/nature05579
creator: Protocols Importer
